info.title: NEUS Public API
info.description: Public interface for creating and resolving cryptographic proofs. Includes health checks, verification polling, verifier discovery, and gate evaluation.

tags.name: Proofs
tags.description: Proof reads, gating checks, and revocation

verifierIds.description: Array of verifier IDs to execute

walletAddress.description: Signer address/public key (EVM: 0x…; Solana: base58 public key)

signature.description: Signature of the NEUS Standard Signing String (EVM: 0x… hex; Solana: base58)

chainId.description: EVM chainId for signature context (legacy EVM mode)

chain.description: CAIP-2 chain reference for universal mode (e.g. eip155:1, solana:mainnet)

signatureMethod.description: Signature method hint for universal mode (eip191, ed25519, ...)

forceZK.description: Deprecated. Bonsai ZK execution is handled internally by specific verifier flows; most deployments ignore this flag.

walletAddress.description: Signer address/public key (EVM: 0x…; Solana: base58 public key)

chain.description: CAIP-2 chain reference for universal mode (e.g. eip155:1, solana:mainnet)

signatureMethod.description: Signature method hint for universal mode (eip191, ed25519, ...)

walletAddress.description: Signer address/public key (EVM: 0x…; Solana: base58 public key)

qHash.description: Proof ID (qHash) to grant access to

viewerWallet.description: Viewer wallet to grant access to

signature.description: Owner signature over Standard Signing String (action=grant_proof_access)

signedTimestamp.description: Unix timestamp in milliseconds (5-minute freshness window enforced server-side)

expiresInSeconds.description: Grant TTL in seconds (default 900, min 60, max 86400)

details.description: Optional structured details (field-level errors, missing fields, etc.)

projections.description: Optional safe projection objects returned by some deployments. Treat as informational only; may be null or omitted.

signerString.description: Exact string to sign with wallet

VerificationData.description: Verification data (verifier-specific). For multi-verifier requests, prefer namespaced payloads: data[verifierId] = { ... }.

anyOf.description: Fallback for custom/verifier-specific payloads not covered by the open-source core verifier set.

VerificationDataNamespaced.description: Multi-verifier payload: keys are verifier IDs and values are that verifier's input object.

content.description: Content being claimed (max 50KB inline; use contentHash + IPFS for larger)

contentHash.description: Pre-computed SHA-256 or keccak256 hash (optional if content provided)

contentType.description: MIME type hint for the content/reference (e.g., image/png, audio/mpeg, application/pdf)

owner.description: Wallet address of the owner (must match request walletAddress)

id.description: Reference identifier (CID, URL, tx hash, chainId:address:tokenId, etc.)

provenance.description: Optional provenance metadata for content origin

aiContext.description: AI generation context (when declaredKind is ai or mixed)

walletAddress.description: Wallet address to match in DNS TXT record (optional; defaults to signer walletAddress)

chainId.description: Chain ID where the contract is deployed (testnet or mainnet; must be supported/configured server-side)

walletAddress.description: Wallet claiming ownership (optional; defaults to signer walletAddress)

ownerAddress.description: NFT owner address (optional). If omitted, defaults to the signed request walletAddress. Deployments may allow delegated subject checks, but the server will not accept subject overrides that break signature binding.

chainId.description: Chain ID where the NFT contract is deployed (testnet or mainnet; must be supported/configured server-side)

blockNumber.description: Optional specific block number for point-in-time ownership checks

ownerAddress.description: Token holder address (optional). If omitted, defaults to the signed request walletAddress. Deployments may allow delegated subject checks, but the server will not accept subject overrides that break signature binding.

minBalance.description: Minimum token balance required (human-readable units, e.g., "10.5")

chainId.description: Chain ID where the token contract is deployed (testnet or mainnet; must be supported/configured server-side)

blockNumber.description: Optional specific block number for point-in-time balance checks

provider.description: Risk assessment provider hint. Known value: webacy.

walletAddress.description: Wallet address to assess (optional; defaults to signer walletAddress)

signature.description: EIP-191 signature from secondary wallet over canonical message

relationshipType.description: Relationship type for identity graph categorization

content.description: Moderation input. For images: base64 content or an IPFS reference (ipfs://<cid>). For text: raw UTF-8 text (size-limited).

contentType.description: MIME type. The server tolerates charset suffixes (e.g., "text/plain; charset=utf-8").

provider.description: Moderation provider override. Known values: google-vision, google-perspective.

description.description: Brief description of what this agent does

capabilities.description: List of capabilities (e.g., trading, posting, payments)

agentId.description: Agent ID for linking to agent-identity proof

scope.description: Scope of delegation (e.g., payments:x402, trading, posting, global)

maxSpend.description: Maximum spend limit in wei (for payments:x402 scope)

allowedPaymentTypes.description: Declared allowed payment protocols for this delegation (e.g., ["x402"]). Metadata-only.

receiptDisclosure.description: Receipt disclosure policy for economic actions (metadata-only). Recommended: summary.

get.description: Check if the API is operational
Infrastructure readiness probe. Returns 200 when critical dependencies are ready.
Infrastructure liveness probe. Returns 200 if the process is alive.

post.description: Create cryptographic proof of ownership, assets, or credentials. Signed request-body using the NEUS Standard Signing String (6-line).

get.description: Check status of a verification by qHash. Public proofs return full data. Private proofs return full data only for the owner when authenticated; otherwise returns a sanitized status.

parameters.name: x-wallet-address
parameters.description: Owner wallet address (optional; used for owner checks)

parameters.name: x-signature
parameters.description: Owner signature over NEUS Standard Signing String with data.action='access_private_proof' and data.qHash (optional; enables private-proof access)

parameters.name: x-signed-timestamp
parameters.description: Signed timestamp (ms) used in the Standard Signing String (optional)

get.description: Get list of public verifier IDs

post.description: Get exact string to sign for verification (troubleshooting)

post.summary: Grant viewer access to a private proof (owner-signed, short-lived)
post.description: Stores a short-lived grant for a viewer wallet to read a private proof record (does not change privacyLevel/publicDisplay). Requires Redis.

get.description: Evaluate whether a wallet satisfies verifier and metadata criteria against PUBLIC/DISCOVERABLE proofs only (privacyLevel=public + publicDisplay=true).

parameters.name: sinceDays
parameters.description: Require proofs created within the last N days

parameters.name: since
parameters.description: Require proofs created after this unix timestamp (ms)

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Max rows to scan (server may clamp)

get.description: Retrieve verification proofs for a wallet address or DID. Public returns discoverable proofs. Owner access to private proofs requires owner authentication.

parameters.name: x-wallet-address
parameters.description: Owner wallet address (optional; must match path address for private access)

parameters.name: x-signature
parameters.description: Owner signature over NEUS Standard Signing String with data.action='access_private_proofs_by_wallet' (optional; enables private proofs)

parameters.name: x-signed-timestamp
parameters.description: Signed timestamp (ms) used in the Standard Signing String (optional)

post.description: Revoke a proof you own using a wallet signature (Standard Signing String).
